summary: Excellent matrix recording captures a stellar first set (Help>Slip>Franklin's, Red Rooster, Loser) and a soaring Dark Star jam in the second set.
review: The matrix source delivers crisp stereo separation and an immersive crowd feel that lets every nuance of the band shine. Reviewers rave about the first‑set highlights – Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower, Red Rooster with killer slide work, and a punchy Loser – and they crown the second‑set Dark Star/Kesey sequence as a night‑defining moment.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviews are five‑star, praising both the musical performance and the matrix recording; no significant negatives were noted.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers highlight superb stereo separation, clear crowd ambience, and a consistently strong mix from start to finish.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is praised for its tight medley work and strong individual songs; second set builds momentum with an average Scarlet/Fire but peaks during the epic Dark Star/Kesey jam.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh's bass shines, especially on the bluesy T‑Thumb style moments.
